skill.name: playwright-skill
skill.description: Complete browser automation with Playwright. Auto-detects dev servers, writes clean test scripts to /tmp. Test pages, fill forms, take screenshots, check responsive design, validate UX, test login flows, and scrape web content.
skill.summary: Browser automation skill for Claude Code using Playwright. Auto-detects dev servers, writes test scripts to /tmp, supports form filling, screenshots, responsive testing, and login flows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate browser automation skill using the Playwright library. The static analyzer flagged many patterns but most are false positives: shell commands in documentation are usage examples, not dangerous execution; network access is required for browser automation; filesystem access is for writing test scripts to /tmp; environment variables are for configuration. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Documentation files contain shell command examples using backticks. These are usage examples for users, not dangerous code execution within the skill itself.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick patterns in markdown files are documentation examples, not actual code execution. The skill itself only executes npm install commands for setup.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Configuration
low_findings.description: Skill reads configuration from environment variables (PW_HEADER_NAME, HEADLESS, etc.). This is legitimate configuration, not credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables are used for skill configuration (browser mode, custom headers), not for exfiltrating credentials.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Script Loading
low_findings.description: Skill uses require() to load Playwright and user test scripts. This is the expected mechanism for a scripting skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: require() is the standard Node.js module loading mechanism. The skill loads Playwright and user-written test scripts, which is the skill's intended purpose.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude Code to automate browser interactions using Playwright, handling dev server detection, test script execution, and comprehensive web testing capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Auto-detect running development servers on common ports (3000, 5173, 8080, etc.)
Write and execute Playwright test scripts to /tmp for clean isolation
Test pages with multiple viewport sizes for responsive design validation
Fill forms, click elements, and handle authentication flows
Take screenshots and capture page content for debugging
Handle cookie banners and common UI elements automatically
limitations: Requires Playwright and browser installation (handled automatically on first use)
Cannot automate browsers on remote machines - only local execution
Some websites may block automated browsers or require additional configuration
Complex CAPTCHAs or anti-bot protections may prevent automation

use_cases.title: Web Developer Testing
use_cases.description: Test web applications locally by auto-detecting dev servers and running automated browser tests across multiple viewport sizes.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers who want to quickly test their applications

use_cases.title: QA Automation
use_cases.description: Create end-to-end tests for web applications, validate login flows, and check responsive design across different devices.

use_cases.title: Web Scraping
use_cases.description: Extract content from websites that require JavaScript rendering, including screenshots and structured data extraction.
use_cases.target_user: Developers needing to scrape JavaScript-heavy websites

prompt_templates.title: Test Local Web App
prompt_templates.prompt: Test my local web application. First detect any running dev servers, then verify the main page loads correctly and check for console errors.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Design Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if my web application looks good on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports. Take screenshots at each size and report any layout issues.

prompt_templates.title: Login Flow Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Test the login flow on [URL]. Fill in the credentials, click login, and verify successful authentication by checking for dashboard or user menu.

prompt_templates.title: Form Submission Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Fill out and submit the contact form on [URL] with test data. Verify the form submits successfully and check for any validation errors.

output_examples.input: Test my local app at localhost:3000
output_examples.output:
Auto-detected dev server on port 3000. Launching browser to test...

Page title: My Web App
Viewport: 1280x720
✓ Page loaded successfully
✓ No console errors detected
✓ Screenshot saved to /tmp/screenshot-2024-01-15.png

Testing across viewports:
- Mobile (375x667): ✓ No layout issues
- Tablet (768x1024): ✓ No layout issues
- Desktop (1280x720): ✓ No layout issues

All viewport tests passed. Screenshots saved to /tmp/

best_practices: Always use parameterized URLs via constants or environment variables for maintainability
Write test scripts to /tmp to keep project directories clean
Use visible browser mode (headless: false) for debugging, switch to headless for CI
Handle async operations properly with waitForSelector and waitForNavigation
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode credentials in test scripts - use environment variables instead
Avoid testing against production websites without authorization
Do not rely solely on sleep/timers - use proper wait conditions for reliable tests
Avoid writing test files to the skill directory - always use /tmp

faq.question: How do I set up Playwright?
faq.answer: Run 'npm run setup' in the skill directory, or the skill will prompt you to install on first use.

faq.question: Can I test external websites?
faq.answer: Yes, but some sites may block automated browsers. Always respect robots.txt and website terms of service.

faq.question: How do I pass custom headers?
faq.answer: Set PW_HEADER_NAME and PW_HEADER_VALUE environment variables, or PW_EXTRA_HEADERS for multiple headers in JSON format.

faq.question: Can I run in headless mode?
faq.answer: Set HEADLESS=false environment variable for visible browser mode. Default is headless for CI environments.

faq.question: Where do test scripts get saved?
faq.answer: Test scripts are written to /tmp/playwright-test-*.js to avoid cluttering your project directory.

faq.question: Does this work with Firefox and WebKit?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports chromium, firefox, and webkit browsers. Install additional browsers with 'npx playwright install firefox'.
